name: Andouille Smoked Sausage in Red Gravy
recipeIngredient: 6 and a half cups pork or beef stock
1 and a half teaspoons cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a Dutch oven over medium heat.
2. Add sausage to the pot, cover, and cook without stirring for about 7 minutes until browned on all sides.
3. Turn the sausage pieces over, sprinkle 2 cups of onions evenly over the top, and cover. Cook for another 7 minutes, or until there is dark brown sediment at the bottom of the pan.
4. Add 3/4 cup of stock to the pot and scrape the bottom using a spatula. Stir in pepper, salt, and stir again.
5. Cover and cook for 2 more minutes, scraping once.
6. Add celery, green peppers, and garlic, cover, and cook for 3 minutes, scraping once.
7. Stir in tomato sauce and cook uncovered for 5 minutes, occasionally scraping the bottom to prevent burning.
8. Add 1/2 cup of chopped onions and cook until large puddles of oil have formed and the tomato mixture thickens (about 8 minutes).
9. Stir only if necessary to prevent sticking.
10. Add parsley and 1/2 cup of green onion tops, then add an additional 3-1/4 cups of stock, scraping occasionally until the liquid forms a thick, dark red gravy.
11. Continue stirring frequently for about 14 more minutes until the gravy is noticeably thicker but still juicy. Remove from heat and serve immediately.
